properties.remark: A shale rock wall slid into Highway 220 and is blocking a lane due to heavy rain.
Boat evacuations being conducted. Other locations in the city also flooded.

Landslide blocking Rock River Road near its intersection with Route 10/Matoaka Road. A tree was brought down with the slide.
rock slide on Matoaka Road.
Howell Street was closed due to flooding from the Greenbrier River.
A rock retaining wall along Duhring Ave near Bramwell has collapsed due to excessive rainfall and is blocking the road.
Rock slide and downed tree. Time of event estimated to be around the time of the other report in Bramwell.
Several roads were closed from south of Alderson to Talcott and even to Hinton due to flooding from the Greenbrier River and its smaller tributaries.

Numerous creeks started to overflow their banks and flood roads throughout the county.
Spring Branch and the South Fork Roanoke River are overflowing their banks and flooding several roads in the town.
Rich Creek overflowed its banks and is flooding both Route 219 and Route 12 in the town of Rich Creek.
Multiple swift water rescues are occurring across the county. Daves Fork, Christians Fork, and the Bluestone River are all overflowing their banks. Numerous roads are impassable due to flooding.

Properties along Edgewater Drive are underwater due to the Clinch River surpassing moderate flood stage.
Several roads are closed and impassable around Roanoke College due to flooding.
Peak Creek flooding over Dora Highway, and also flooding at Heritage Park. Water starting to come out the banks in the town of Pulaski with water over Main Street and Walnut Ave.

*** 1 FATAL *** Corrects previous flash flood report from 1 ESE Kimberling. A vehicle with a single occupant was pushed off of Kimberling Road into deeper water as Kimberling Creek flooded at least 2 feet over the roadway. The occupant attempted to abandon the vehicle, but was swept away by the flooding. Time of occurrence is estimated.

Severe flooding along Grove Hill Road between White Church Road and the Botetourt/Craig County line. Catawba Creek out of its banks. Extensive road damage. 6 homes cutoff from access along Grove Hill Road between White Church and Lees Gap Road with 20 people sheltering in place. 3 vehicles were abandoned after they stalled in high water. 3 to 5 inches of rain fell near the Botetourt and Craig County line in 2 hours.

Significant flash flooding occurred on Wards Mill Branch creek, as several structures became surrounded by fast moving water. This resulted in swift water rescues of nine people and four pets in the 100 block of Old Quaker Road. There were also evacuations of a nearby mobile home park on Macy Drive. A downstream river gage, Chestnut Creek at Galax, rose over 4 feet. No injuries were reported.

An intense thunderstorm moved across southern Halifax County, Virginia during the afternoon of September 4th. A tornado formed within an area of straight-line damaging winds, touching down just west of Cherry Hill Church Road, then traveling east of and lifting before reaching Arthur Ware Trail. The tornado snapped and uprooted 50-100 trees. The tornadic winds damaged the outer wall of a modular home just below the roof line. The tornado touched down at 451 PM, traveled just over a quarter of mile and lifted at 452 PM.
